nodes.label: Supabase and GitHub Pages Setup Guide
nodes.norm_label: supabase and github pages setup guide

nodes.label: Database-First Capacity Upgrade
nodes.rationale: The additive database migration and affected Edge Functions are deployed before the frontend so the new application cannot request an RPC that is not yet available.

nodes.label: Number-Neutral Capacity Error
nodes.rationale: Edge Functions preserve the database error code while avoiding a duplicated numeric maximum in user-facing error text.

nodes.label: Module activation in a later milestone
nodes.source_location: module card body text: Dieses Modul wird in einem späteren Meilenstein aktiviert.
nodes.norm_label: module activation in a later milestone

nodes.label: Guarded Browser Storage Boundary
nodes.rationale: Browser storage access is resolved behind a guard so rendering survives an unavailable localStorage getter, while failed fallback writes still surface the existing persistence warning.

nodes.label: On-Primary Contrast Mapping
nodes.source_location: RED/GREEN evidence: On-primary contrast and mappings
nodes.rationale: Primary controls and brand marks use a dedicated on-primary token so both themes meet at least 4.5:1 contrast, including hover states.

nodes.label: Document-Level Modal Stack Focus Containment
nodes.source_location: Accessibility follow-up root causes and independent review follow-up
nodes.rationale: A document-level modal stack blocks focus and pointer actions outside the topmost dialog and retains restoration chains so nested or removed dialogs restore focus safely.

nodes.label: Dedicated Focus Ring Token
nodes.rationale: Focus visibility uses a theme-specific token rather than the primary hover color so outlines maintain at least 3:1 contrast against adjacent surfaces.

nodes.label: Device-Local Personalization
nodes.rationale: Theme, manual desktop-sidebar state, and mobile-tab choices stay on the device and require neither database storage nor secrets.

nodes.label: Static React and Supabase Architecture
nodes.rationale: The browser remains a static GitHub Pages application while Supabase owns authentication, data, and privileged server operations.

nodes.label: Transactional Account Capacity Enforcement
nodes.rationale: PostgreSQL counts invited and active profiles plus pending reservations under an advisory transaction lock so browser checks cannot bypass the ten-slot limit.

nodes.label: Secure Bootstrap and Invitation Edge Functions
nodes.norm_label: secure bootstrap and invitation edge functions

nodes.label: CI, Pages Deployment, and Secret Scanning
nodes.norm_label: ci, pages deployment, and secret scanning

nodes.label: Typed Device Preference Repository and Provider
nodes.norm_label: typed device preference repository and provider

nodes.label: Deterministic Monday-First Month Model
nodes.rationale: A pure native-Date model produces a stable 42-day grid and anchors month shifts to day one, preventing short-month overflow and keeping tests deterministic.

nodes.label: Calendar Unit, Route, and Browser Verification
nodes.norm_label: calendar unit, route, and browser verification

nodes.label: One Source of Truth
nodes.rationale: A record created in one area should appear automatically in every relevant view, avoiding duplicated domain state.

nodes.label: Private by Default
nodes.rationale: Content remains visible only to its owner unless the owner explicitly shares it with viewer or editor permission.

nodes.label: No Silent AI Writes
nodes.source_location: Product goal and AI confirmation workflow
nodes.rationale: AI mutations are represented as expiring, human-readable proposals and are applied only after user confirmation and permission/version revalidation.

nodes.label: RLS and Edge Function Security Model
nodes.norm_label: rls and edge function security model

nodes.label: React, Supabase, and GitHub Pages PWA Architecture
nodes.norm_label: react, supabase, and github pages pwa architecture

nodes.label: Advisory Transaction Lock for Admission
nodes.rationale: Reservation and restoration acquire the same PostgreSQL advisory transaction lock before counting, preserving serialized atomic admission under concurrent requests.

nodes.label: Browser Capacity State Is Advisory
nodes.rationale: The UI uses database-returned occupied and maximum values for display and disabling, but stale clients cannot bypass atomic server enforcement.

nodes.label: Dark and Light Semantic Theme Tokens
nodes.norm_label: dark and light semantic theme tokens

nodes.label: Preference Storage Failure Recovery
nodes.rationale: Invalid or unavailable storage falls back to complete defaults; failed writes preserve live session state and produce a neutral persistence warning.

nodes.label: Stable Monday-First 42-Day Model
nodes.rationale: A fixed six-week grid keeps the month layout stable, shows adjacent-month days, and anchors navigation to the first day of the visible month.

nodes.label: Read-Only Semantic Month Table
nodes.rationale: Because dates are read-only rather than selectable, native table semantics provide the appropriate accessible structure instead of an interactive ARIA grid.

nodes.label: No External Calendar Data Constraint
nodes.rationale: Calendar V1 deliberately excludes events, persistence, synchronization, Supabase, and network access, so it needs no loading or network error state.
